title: Ports, Protocols, Services, and Traffic Types · Protocol Clinic: Net Secure Insecure Services
summary: Build and diagnose a service-access matrix from client, firewall, resolver, server, and capture evidence. Planned artifact: Add and verify the net secure insecure services increment in a verified protocol and service matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare secure and insecure service alternatives such as SSH or Telnet, HTTPS or HTTP, LDAPS or LDAP, and SFTP or FTP.
Use N10-009 standard ports for file transfer, remote administration, mail, DNS, DHCP, web, time, monitoring, directory, database, desktop, and voice services.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Build Net Secure Insecure Services evidence
steps.instruction: Append a safe investigation headed "# Evidence: net-secure-insecure-services-1e3dg4" with a concrete hypothesis, one read-only network command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Net Secure Insecure Services into observable performance evidence.
